---
title: "Hands-on: Version Control with Git"
format:
  html:
    toc-depth: 4
execute:
  eval: false
---

# Exercise

- Open a Github codespace from the forked workshop repository. Visit the [instructions](../../instructions/index.qmd) page to learn how to do this.

- Within the codespace, adjust the README file by adding your name, ORCID, or any other information you find relevant. Save the changes.

- **Commit** the changes. Provide a commit message.
    - You can do so either using the Terminal, or using the built-in Git intergration (visible in the left-hand pane).

- **Push** the changes to your remote repository on Github.

- Verify the changes on Github in the README.

- Optional: further personalize the repository to your liking.

# Additional Resources

-   The Turing Way — Version Control: <https://the-turing-way.netlify.app/reproducible-research/vcs>
-   Happy Git with R: <https://happygitwithr.com>
